skill.name: ux-audit
skill.description: Perform structured UX audits for pages, components, and product features using usability heuristics and design best practices.
skill.summary: Evaluate interfaces with a repeatable UX audit framework and prioritized recommendations.
skill.author: Dami-Randle

security_audit.summary: Static command execution findings are false positives from Markdown fences and inline names in a documentation-only skill. Static weak cryptography findings are also false positives from ordinary UX prose, with no cryptographic code or malicious instructions found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Are Not Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md lines 60, 80, 85, and 86. Lines 60 and 80 are a fenced Markdown output example, while lines 85 and 86 are inline references to related tools. No executable shell command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged characters appear only in Markdown formatting and inline tool names. There is no script, interpreter call, or user-controlled command execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md lines 3, 14, 30, 49, and 55. These lines contain UX audit descriptions and checklist text, not hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural language guidance for UX evaluation. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or any other cryptographic operation.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear evidence before changing a user interface. This skill reviews pages, components, and flows with a structured UX checklist and prioritized recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Evaluates interfaces against Nielsen usability heuristics.
Checks visual hierarchy, grouping, spacing, and prominence.
Reviews interaction clarity, feedback, loading states, and empty states.
Assesses labels, calls to action, terminology, and content clarity.
Produces summary scores, critical issues, recommendations, and positive patterns.
limitations: Does not replace direct user research or usability testing.
Does not include automated accessibility scanning by itself.
Requires screenshots, descriptions, or product context for accurate review.
Does not validate analytics, conversion data, or production behavior.

use_cases.title: Pre-release feature review
use_cases.description: Audit a new page or component before launch and identify usability risks that need attention.

use_cases.title: Backlog prioritization
use_cases.description: Turn broad usability concerns into ranked issues with effort estimates and practical recommendations.

use_cases.title: Implementation design QA
use_cases.description: Compare a built interface against UX principles before handing work to stakeholders or testers.

prompt_templates.title: Basic page audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have a screenshot or page description.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the UX of this page using the ux-audit skill. Focus on usability, visual hierarchy, interaction clarity, and content.

prompt_templates.title: Component usability review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when reviewing a single component or modal.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component with the ux-audit skill. Identify friction, unclear states, confusing labels, and missing feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritized release checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before release planning or design signoff.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a UX audit for this feature and rank issues by priority, user impact, and implementation effort.

prompt_templates.title: Comparative flow audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when evaluating a complete user journey.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this multi-step flow with the ux-audit skill. Compare each step against usability heuristics and recommend changes for the full journey.

output_examples.input: Audit a checkout page before release.
output_examples.output: A structured UX audit with an overall rating, heuristic scores, critical issues, prioritized recommendations, and positive patterns.

output_examples.input: Review a settings modal for usability problems.
output_examples.output: A focused component review that highlights unclear labels, missing feedback, error prevention gaps, and quick design improvements.
A journey-level assessment that flags friction across steps and recommends changes to improve clarity, control, and completion.

best_practices: Provide screenshots, interaction states, and product goals with each audit request.
Ask for priority, impact, and effort when recommendations must feed a backlog.
Pair the audit with accessibility review and user testing for stronger evidence.
anti_patterns: Do not treat heuristic scores as proof of user behavior.
Do not request a broad audit without sharing the target user or task.
Do not use the output as a substitute for accessibility compliance testing.

faq.question: What does this skill evaluate?
faq.answer: It evaluates usability heuristics, visual hierarchy, interaction feedback, loading and empty states, labels, and calls to action.

faq.question: Can it review screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use screenshots or detailed interface descriptions when enough context is provided.

faq.question: Does it perform accessibility testing?
faq.answer: No. It can notice accessibility concerns, but it recommends a dedicated WCAG review for compliance.

faq.question: What output should I expect?
faq.answer: Expect a summary score, heuristic scores, critical issues, prioritized recommendations, and positive patterns.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Product designers, product managers, and frontend engineers can use it for design QA and release readiness.

faq.question: Does it run code or external commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation-only and guides the assistant through a UX audit process.
